resources.string: Use your tablet as a second PC monitor
FPS, latency and bitrate overlay during stream
Invalid IP. Use 127.0.0.1, localhost, or an IPv4 like 192.168.1.62

How to connect via USB (recommended)
1. Install ADB on your PC and connect the tablet with a USB cable.
2. Run in a terminal on your PC:
  adb reverse tcp:9001 tcp:9001
3. Start the host app on your PC.
4. Leave the IP field as 127.0.0.1 and press Connect.

How to connect via Wi-Fi
1. Make sure your PC and tablet are on the same Wi-Fi network.
2. Find your PC\'s local IP: open a terminal and run ipconfig (Windows).
  Look for "IPv4 Address", e.g. 192.168.1.50.
3. Enter that IP in the IP Address field and press Connect.

Display modes
• Mirror – the tablet shows exactly what is on your PC screen.
• Extended – the tablet acts as a second independent monitor. Windows sees it as a separate display.

Target monitor (advanced)
Only relevant in Extended mode if you have more than two monitors.
Leave it empty and the app will choose automatically.
If you need a specific one: open Windows Settings → System → Display and note the number shown on each screen (1, 2, 3…). Enter that number here.

Performance HUD
Shows FPS, latency and bitrate in the top-right corner while streaming.
Toggle it with the switch in this panel before connecting.

Black screen on Netflix / streaming apps?
This is normal. Windows protects DRM content (Netflix, Prime, Disney+) from screen capture. There is no workaround — it is a Windows security policy.

Tip: to stop the stream
Press the Back button on your tablet.
Note: DRM content (Netflix, Prime, etc.) may appear black — this is HDCP/Widevine protection by Windows.
